homepage.text_excerpt: Sharron Davies Skip to content Sharron Davies Home Blog Biography Gallery Contact External Links Sharron Davies Training Unfair Play – Buy Now Paragon Structures Arthosamid X @sharrond62 X page opens in new window Mail page opens in new window close Home Blog Biography Gallery Contact External Links Sharron Davies Training Unfair Play – Buy Now Paragon Structures Arthosamid X @sharrond62 Welcome to SharronDavies.com Women's Sports Union UK A not-for-profit organisation of like-minded people passionate about fair and safe sport for women and girls. Women’s Sports Union UK Sharron Davies Training Train anytime, anywhere for just 4×15 mins a week, all levels of ability with Sharron from her own home. Sharron Davies Training Unfair Play A book Sharron’s extremely proud of, explaining the challenges to women’s sport over the decades & today, using common sense, facts & the peer reviewed science. Shortlisted for the prestigious William Hill sports book of the year. Amazon: Buy Now Squid Go Fast, drug-free relief from muscle soreness, joint pain, and swelling. Ideal for arthritis, sports recovery, injuries, and post-surgery. Squid Go Arthosamid A ground breaking new injection that’s Sharron’s had that can relieve osteoarthritis knee pain, stopping that vicious circle of inactivity & anti inflammatories. Arthosamid Biography Download photos & Sharron’s biography here for any work engagements. Read my bio X (formerly known as Twitter) Come say hello & see what Sharron’s up to. @sharrond62 Get in touch Whether you want to ask a question, book her for an interview, wish to work with her – why not contact Sharron Davies MBE? Heidi Vistisen, policy manager at Leap Sport Scotland, which promotes LGBTI participation in sport, made the remarks during a health committee… Read more May 25 2023 Sharron on GB News In the News , Latest By Sharron Davies 25 May 2023 Feminist groups have expressed shock after it was revealed that a Parkrun women’s record is still held by trans-athlete and criminal, Lauren Jeska. But should transgender women be excluded because of their physical advantage or are they being treated unfairly? Former Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies believes the gender category you perform in, should be based… Read more Apr 1 2023 In praise of Sharron Davies In the News , Latest By Sharron Davies 1 April 2023 It’s been quite a while since we celebrated any of Sharron Davies’s considerable achievements in the pool – well, a bronze and a silver in 1990 at Auckland was the last time – but I would bet a box full of brand-new Speedos to a secondhand pair of goggles that nothing has made her prouder… Read more Mar 25 2023 Thank you for standing up for female athletes In the News , Latest By Sharron Davies 25 March 2023 ‘Thank you for standing up for female athletes’: Ex-Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies praises World Athletics for voting to exclude transgender athletes from the women’s category Ex-swimmer Sharron Davies said the move shows woman are worthy of fair sport Former British Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies has come out in support of World Athletics’ decision to exclude transgender… Read more Mar 24 2023 Transgender women banned by World Athletics In the News , Latest By Sharron Davies 24 March 2023 Plymouth-born Olympic swimmer hails decision to ban transgender women from competing in female international events Plymouth-born Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies has hailed the landmark decision by World Athletics to ban transgender women from competing in female international events. pages.text_excerpt: Building better facilities is a priority – Sharron Davies Skip to content Sharron Davies Home Blog Biography Gallery Contact External Links Sharron Davies Training Unfair Play – Buy Now Paragon Structures Arthosamid X @sharrond62 X page opens in new window Mail page opens in new window close Home Blog Biography Gallery Contact External Links Sharron Davies Training Unfair Play – Buy Now Paragon Structures Arthosamid X @sharrond62 Building better facilities is a priority You are here: Home Latest Building better facilities is a… Your article on the employment crisis in the sector (HCM 3 2022, page 54 or www.hcmmag.com/staffing) raised valid points about what needs to be done to address staff shortages. I particularly applaud Jo Cherrett’s call for an honest conversation about the living wage. This especially applies to swimming teachers. Two members of my family have left swimming teacher jobs because of poor pay. With a national shortage of 8,000 swimming teachers (Swim England 2021), addressing pay will go a long way to attracting and retaining these professionals at a time when their skills are needed more than ever. Children have already lost two years of swimming; we can’t afford for them to miss any more because of a lack of teachers.
